title: USAC Gasoline demand looking better than margins are suggesting
section_path: USAC Gasoline demand looking better than margins are suggesting
section_path_text: USAC Gasoline demand looking better than margins are suggesting
summary:
Main
Global gasoline margins have weakened recently despite moving closer toward driving/gasoline season. We look at the recent downturn and what it means in the context of the changing refining dynamics in the Atlantic Basin. By Pamela Munger
US gasoline margins have started off the gasoline season below last year's levels by nearly $3/bbl and the transatlantic arb (on paper) has been slow to open despite relatively low freight rates ( TC2 ) and low gasoline stocks in PADD 1 (EIA).
